story: Under federal rule, colleges must leave grads better off or lose financial aid
title: New Rule: Colleges Must Deliver
script: 108 commenters are debating a new federal rule. It matters because colleges must now prove their value to students and the government. The rule requires them to leave graduates better off financially or risk losing access to financial aid, a significant portion of their funding. This change has sparked a heated discussion about the role of higher education and student debt. As colleges scramble to comply, one question remains, what will happen to the ones that fail to meet this new standard?
